content.problemStatement:
This time, Chef has given you an array AAA containing NNN elements.
He had also asked you to answer MMM of his questions. Each question sounds like: "How many inversions will the array AAA contain, if we swap the elements at the ithi^{th}ith and the jthj^{th}jth positions?".
The inversion is such a pair of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) that i<ji < ji<j and Ai>AjA_{i} > A_{j}Ai​>Aj​.


Input Format

The first line contains two space separated integers NNN and MMM - the number of integers in the array AAA and the number of questions respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_{1}, A_{2}, \ldots, A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​, respectively.
Each of next MMM lines describes a question by two integers iii and jjj - the 1-based indices of the numbers we'd like to swap in this question.


Output Format
Output MMM lines. Output the answer to the ithi^{th}ith question of the ithi^{th}ith line.
constraints: Mind that we don't actually swap the elements, we only answer "what if" questions, so the array doesn't change after the question.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Inversions for the first case: (2, 3), (2, 4), (2, 5), (3, 5), (4, 5).
Inversions for the second case: (1, 3), (1, 5), (2, 3), (2, 4), (2,5), (4, 5).
In the third case the array looks like 1 2 3 3 4 5 and there are no inversions.
